mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS anchored near the zero lower bound (0.15% as of April 2013), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, mechanically supporting valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns should be positively biased relative to historical unconditional average due to persistent yield-seeking flows into equities.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / quantitative easing regime
failure_condition: If 20-day forward positive-return rate during FEDFUNDS<0.5% periods is not statistically higher (p>0.05, t-test) than the full-sample base rate of 65.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE below a 0.2 percentage-point threshold over a rolling 3-month window signals improving labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE declined from 7.9 to 7.5 over prior months as of April 2013
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE declines are not significantly higher than non-triggered periods (t-test p>0.05), or the sign flips negative in more than 40% of triggered instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects simultaneous strengthening of durable-goods and consumption demand channels, a combination that historically precedes broad-based earnings upgrades and positive equity momentum over a one-month horizon. Neither series alone is sufficient; the co-movement is required to confirm broad demand strength rather than a single-sector effect.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3m avg growth > 0% AND RSAFS 3m avg growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value=1036.0 (revised 1010.0) at 2013-03-01 indicating housing recovery
failure_condition: If joint-trigger periods do not show 20-day forward positive-return rate exceeding the single-series trigger rates by at least 5 percentage points, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window, reject the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) sustained trend growth in an environment of low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals healthy supply-side expansion without overheating risk, supporting a benign macro backdrop that produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. This mechanism is specific to low-inflation expansions and is not expected to hold during high-inflation or stagflationary regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=232.282 near flat vintage_value=232.34 indicating low inflation as of March 2013
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with low, stable inflation
failure_condition: If the positive INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically during periods when CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is below 2.5%, or reverses sign, reject the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero while PAYEMS shows a strong monthly gain exceeding 175,000 jobs, markets interpret the data as increasing the probability of near-term QE tapering, producing short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as investors reprice liquidity withdrawal risk.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value=135474.0 revised to 135873.0 as of 2013-04-01
regime_hint: late quantitative easing / pre-taper anticipation period
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns after strong-payrolls triggers are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong PAYEMS growth and declining UNRATE signal a tightening labor market that raises market expectations of future monetary policy normalization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as discount-rate expectations rise. The effect requires both signals jointly; PAYEMS growth alone without UNRATE decline reflects labor force expansion rather than tightening.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3m avg change > 150,000 AND UNRATE falls > 0.1pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=7.5 vintage vs 7.6 revised at 2013-04-01 with concurrent PAYEMS growth
regime_hint: labor market tightening phase ahead of policy normalization
failure_condition: If joint-trigger 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline, or interaction effect fails to outperform either single-series signal by Rank IC margin >0.02, reject the interaction hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer demand, which is incorporated quickly into near-term equity pricing, producing a positive but short-lived S&P 500 return effect over the subsequent trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value=419027.0 revised to 404231.0 as of 2013-04-01
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with trailing RSAFS growth (Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed contraction in industrial production, defined as two consecutive months of negative INDPRO growth, signals an emerging supply-side slowdown that foreshadows weaker corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth negative for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=98.7439 revised to 99.1065 as of 2013-04-01
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns following two-month INDPRO contraction triggers are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), or positive-return frequency exceeds 55% in triggered instances, reject.
